author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Yoga Ball Rolling class
content:
Ball Rolling (less experience ) 
Great way to begin a beautiful day on Block Island. This class will use balls to engage in deep tissue self massage. Suitable for all levels, we will breathe and roll to release chronic tension and more recent tight spots.  
Class always ends with deep relaxation.
 

Fees
​Drop–in     $15.00
8 Classes    $100.00

author: Austin Crawford
title: Seining in Old Harbor
summary: Meet at Mary D. Park. Come seine with us and observe the abundance of marine life that exists within the Old Harbor break wall in walking distance to the ferry. Wear shoes that can get wet.
content:
Meet at Mary D. Park. Come seine with us and observe the abundance of marine life that exists within the Old Harbor break wall in walking distance to the ferry. Wear shoes that can get wet.

title: Salt Marsh Creatures James Stover Exploration

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Yoga Beginner
content:
Beginner no experience ) 
This class is for everyBODY. 
Gentle practice with detailed guidance to release physical and mental stress; promote relaxation and strengthen and tone the muscles, joints and spine.
 
Beautiful introduction for the first time practitioner or a restorative class for all practitioners.

author: Austin Crawford
title: URI Gardening with the Masters Tour
summary: This garden and property are a 20-year ongoing labor of love, featuring hundreds of perennial shrubs and flowers, all maintained by the owners. Rustic nautical and farm implements frame garden spaces throughout this two-acre property on Block Island. At the entrance, visitors are greeted by pocket gardens of plants native to the Northeast. A large perennial installation along the driveway includes a four-season display of trees, shrubs, and flowers. Behind the home, a walled vegetable garden with raised beds and potted herbs is ringed by the gardeners’ favorite perennials that deer can’t reach! Other interesting areas include a moss-covered “secret path” and a separate composting area. Eighteenth century stone walls surround the property, and stone walkways meander through the gardens. All the plants and trees are carefully selected to withstand Block Island’s unique environment, and must withstand high winds, heavy deer browse and salt spray.
content:
This garden and property are a 20-year ongoing labor of love, featuring hundreds of perennial shrubs and flowers, all maintained by the owners. Rustic nautical and farm implements frame garden spaces throughout this two-acre property on Block Island. At the entrance, visitors are greeted by pocket gardens of plants native to the Northeast. A large perennial installation along the driveway includes a four-season display of trees, shrubs, and flowers. Behind the home, a walled vegetable garden with raised beds and potted herbs is ringed by the gardeners’ favorite perennials that deer can’t reach! Other interesting areas include a moss-covered “secret path” and a separate composting area. Eighteenth century stone walls surround the property, and stone walkways meander through the gardens. All the plants and trees are carefully selected to withstand Block Island’s unique environment, and must withstand high winds, heavy deer browse and salt spray.

Located jardin de mer

10:00 – 4:00 p.m. both days, rain or shine

author: BI Chamber
title: Autumn Berry Walk: James Stover Exploration Series
content:
Meet at Nathan Mott Park

Join us for a walk at Nathan Mott Park focusing on the variety of fall and early winter berries. Email Kim to sign up: kim.gaffett@tnc.org

All are welcome to attend The James Stover Block Island Exploration Series

Discover the fascinating nature of Block Island. Guided monthly walks for families (with kids of all ages) and individuals explore areas and species special to Block Island. These walks are funded by friends of James Stover (1996-2003), in memory of a boy who loved exploring Block Island.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Community Blood Drive
summary: Here are some quick things to keep in mind:
content:
Here are some quick things to keep in mind:

  • If you would like to make an appointment, visit www.ribc.org/drives or call 401-453-8383. Enter sponsor code 2639. Walk-ins can only be accepted if safe social distancing allows at the time of arrival.
  • Anyone 17 years or older can donate. There is no upper age limit to donating. Sixteen-year-old donors need a parent’s permission slip that can be found at ribc.org/permission.
  • Please bring your driver’s license or other form of identification.
  • Concerning tickborne Illnesses: If you have had Lyme disease in the past but you were diagnosed more than 30 days ago and are feeling fine now, you may donate blood. You cannot donate blood if you are still taking antibiotics. If you've had a positive test for Babesiosis, you may donate two years after the positive test. Please contact RIBC’s medical team at 401-453-8307 ormedicalteam@ribc.org if you have any questions.
  • For most medical conditions, including cancer or heart attacks, you can donate once you’re feeling well and after a period of time. Most medications do not disqualify you from blood donation. Learn more atribc.org/giving-101/facts-faqs/ or if you have a specific question, please contact our medical team at 401-453-8307 or medicalteam@ribc.org.

Whether you are a year-round resident or a guest on the island, please consider giving. The need for donations here and across the country is great right now. One donation from you can help save three lives. From beginning to end, the process will take about one hour

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Lions Club Golf Tournament
summary: Block Island Lions Club Golf Tournament will be on Monday, July 11 at 10 a.m.
content:
Block Island Lions Club Golf Tournament will be on Monday, July 11 at 10 a.m.
at the Richmond Country Club. Interstate Navigation has generously donated the
round-trip boat tickets. Please contact Jon McCusker at (401) 218-3476 or Michele
Phelan at (401) 741-6577 for details and to sign up. Cost is $120. All proceeds go
towards scholarships and educational enrichment programs.
